subtitle: Ball of foot pain — forefoot overload

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Common in forefoot runners and those transitioning to lower-drop shoes

mild.description: Burning or aching under the ball of the foot during or after runs
mild.can_run_note: Yes — add forefoot cushioning and check shoe stack height
why: Metatarsal heads absorbing excess load from forefoot running pattern
Thin midsole or low-drop shoe increasing forefoot pressure
Tight toe flexors limiting normal forefoot load distribution
relief: Metatarsal pad in shoe — placed behind the ball of the foot
Ice ball of foot for 15 min after runs
Toe spreads and foot doming exercises
Avoid barefoot walking on hard floors
prevention: Gradual transition to lower-drop or minimal shoes — minimum 12 weeks
Forefoot strength work: toe spreads, towel scrunches
Sufficient cushioning under the metatarsal heads for road running

moderate.description: Persistent forefoot pain, tender to press on metatarsal heads
moderate.can_run_note: Easy pace only with extra forefoot cushioning
why: Stress reaction in one or more metatarsal shafts
Morton's neuroma — nerve thickening between the 3rd and 4th toes
Capsulitis of the lesser toe joints from chronic overload
relief: Wider toe box shoe to reduce nerve compression
prevention: Wide toe box footwear for daily wear
Assess running form — reduce forefoot strike intensity

severe.description: Sharp forefoot pain, burning between toes, cannot run
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — possible metatarsal stress fracture or neuroma
why: Metatarsal stress fracture requiring imaging and offloading
Morton's neuroma requiring injection or surgical consultation
Severe capsulitis or plantar plate tear
relief: X-ray or MRI to rule out stress fracture
Offloading boot if stress fracture confirmed
prevention: 6–8 weeks non-weight-bearing or boot for stress fracture

see_physio_if: Burning or electric sensation between the toes
Specific tender spot on one metatarsal shaft — possible stress fracture
Pain that does not improve with rest
